candidate_problems.text: Determine whether the grief/depression distinction can be captured by explanatory structure in cases of non-bereavement depression, chronic grief, and culturally variable mourning practices.
Explain cases where depression is tightly anchored to a real loss or circumstance, which challenges the claim that depression is characteristically generalized rather than reality-tracking.
Specify what observable markers would distinguish a painful but reality-tracking reorganization of meaning from a self-sealing depressive explanatory regime in clinical practice.
